location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week in-person

level_signals: 4+ years of direct product management experience
5+ years of experience in brokerage, market-making, or institutional trading
Base pay range Zone 1: $166,000–$195,000, consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of direct product management experience building high-quality platform products
5+ years of experience in brokerage, market-making, or institutional trading
Familiarity using research, experimentation and product analytics to connect platform decision making with customer experience
Ability to focus on a problem at hand while thoughtfully considering overarching company strategy
Openness to all solutions and ability to build conviction in a solution based on evidence
Passion for financial products and improving people's financial lives

scope.surface: Trading Platform — routing, reporting, order and execution management, fractional and notional trading, performance monitoring and management
primary_metrics_inferred: Expansion of trading capabilities (e.g. 24 Hour Market, fractional/notional orders)
core_responsibilities: Lead a product team across engineering, data science, trading, execution services, design and research focused on optimizing and expanding our trading services.
Establish a product vision and shared set of priorities across the team and the brokerage to optimize and expand our trading capabilities.

Deliver efficient, flexible, expert friendly interfaces that help trading, engineering, and execution services operate all trading sessions, troubleshoot problems, and assist customers with all aspects of trading.
Work cross-functionally to ship high-impact features that increase efficiency and improve customers' experience.

compensation.equity_form: equity ownership mentioned as part of compensation package

green_flags: Compensation ranges explicitly disclosed across three geographic zones
Clear product surface with named existing features (fractional/notional orders, 24 Hour Market)
Bonus, equity, and 401(k) matching all mentioned
Strong mission alignment language for candidates passionate about democratizing finance
red_flags: Dual 4+ year PM and 5+ year brokerage/trading experience requirements are both hard — finding candidates who satisfy both is a high bar
Hybrid policy requires at least 3 days/week in-person; limited to NYC, Menlo Park, or Chicago — eliminates remote candidates
Visa sponsorship is not addressed in the JD — unclear for international candidates
No explicit nice-to-haves listed, making it hard to gauge flexibility on requirements

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, years, and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; JD says 'lead a team' but no direct reports mentioned)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
compensation.base_range_usd uses Zone 1 (highest range) as primary; Zone 2 is $146,000–$172,000 and Zone 3 is $129,000–$152,000
ambiguities: The 4+ years PM experience and 5+ years brokerage/trading experience are listed under 'What you bring' without explicit 'required' vs 'preferred' labels; treated as hard requirements given unconditional phrasing and seniority level.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; set to 'unclear'.
It is ambiguous whether the PM experience and brokerage experience must be in separate roles or can overlap (i.e., PM at a brokerage counts for both).
